answers: Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems built by colonies of tiny coral polyps. They support 25% of all marine species despite covering less than 1% of the ocean floor.
Coral reef holo samudrer niche coral polyp diye toiri ecosystem. Great Barrier Reef holo prithibir shobcheye boro coral reef — Australia te.
Coral reefs protect coastlines from storms, support fisheries, provide medicines, and generate tourism revenue. They're sometimes called the rainforests of the sea due to their biodiversity.
Coral bleaching happens when stressed corals expel their symbiotic algae, turning white. Rising ocean temperatures are the main cause, and bleached corals can die if conditions don't improve.
